questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 106-4-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: Which state is the tornado most likely to strike in May?
options: Florida ▲ 閱讀下文後,作答第 37-41 題 Absallah al-Mouallimi, the Saudi ambassador to the UN, cheerfully announced a small step toward gender equality in Saudi Arabia after a decree was issued giving women the right to drive. This change will take effect in June 2018. Before the change, women in the ultra-conservative kingdom who defied the ban in protest would often go to jail. “It is amazing,” said a Saudi university professor, who joined in the first protest against the ban in 1990. She was arrested after her driving around the Saudi capital, Riyadh. She has been waiting for the change for a long time. There were many explanations for the driving ban over the years. Some said that it was simply inappropriate for women to drive or that men didn’t know how to react when they were sitting next to a female driver. Many others believed that allowing women to drive would lead to the collapse of the Saudi family. It was hard to imagine this, yet still others explained that driving would harm women’s ovaries, the parts of female body organs that contain eggs, even though they could not find the evidence. This change is embraced by right groups and Saudi activists. Although Saudi women still need permissions from a male guardian—an official guardian, typically a father, brother, uncle or husband—to travel, get a job, marry or divorce, this is still a welcome move toward modernity.

Women’s driving may lead to the collapse of their family.
People in Saudi Arabia culture just think it’s not a good idea for women to drive.
